creator: cy@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Slawomir Wojciech Wojtczak from comment #4)

>
> These packages - in that particular configuration - are needed to attach
> FreeBSD to FreeIPA/IDM.

FreeIPA works just like Active Directory. Authentication is separate from 
metadata like UID/GID and Windows SID.

>
> Details here:
> - https://vermaden.wordpress.com/2022/11/17/connect-freebsd-freeipa-idm/
> -
> https://vermaden.wordpress.com/2023/03/29/connect-freebsd-13-2-to-freeipa-idm/
>
> I turned on these options:
>
>     make -C ${PORTS}/security/krb5
>     [x] LDAP

Why? Unless it's serving authentication via LDAP, like A/D, you don't need 
this.

And updates are made using the kadmin protocol.

>
>     make -C ${PORTS}/security/cyrus-sasl2-gssapi config
>     (*) GSSAPI_MIT

This you may need but GSSAPI in the base FreeBSD will communicate with MIT 
KRB5. Only the kadmin protocol is different between MIT and Hiemdal. 
cyrus-sasl2 is just another client.

>
>     make -C ${PORTS}/net/openldap26-client config
>     [x] GSSAPI

Why do you need this?  OpenLDAP is used to serve NSS data like UIDs, GIDs, 
hostnames, etc., just like NIS and NIS+ used to back in the day. Active 
Directory uses both.

I recall talking to Paul Hill at LISA2000 at the MIT KRB5 SIG. He was 
lamenting that M$ was making incompatible changes to the the Kerberos 
protocol.

>
>     make -C ${PORTS}/security/sudo config
>     [ ] PAM
>     [x] SSSD
>     (*) GSSAPI_MIT

I use PAM.

	GSSAPI_BASE    : off
	GSSAPI_HEIMDAL : off
	GSSAPI_MIT     : off
	PAM            : on

pam_krb5 in base and the pam_krb5 port work well with MIT KRB5.

>
> > What you have done by turning on all options is unsupported.
>
> OK.
>
> > I could remove the LDAP option from the MIT KRB5 ports.
>
> Please do not do that - do not remove LDAP support.
>
> I will not use Poudriere then for this set of packages.

I build everything using my own poudriere. You can create circular 
dependencies by hand but you cannot update them using poudriere, portmaster or portupgrade. None of these can handle circular dependencies because they 
assume nothing has been installed yet. You can build with circular 
dependencies because you can build the first port, i.e. krb5 or 
openldap-client, then build it again after everything else is 
installed. When building by hand, it's a cheat but it works. You can never upgrade it though.

This is not a limitation of the ports build infrastructure. It cannot know that it must build the first port, whichever one is chosen, without the option, them build it again with after.
